title: ConvertKit vs Campaign Monitor (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Compare ConvertKit and Campaign Monitor. See pricing, features, and which email marketing tool fits your needs.
h1: ConvertKit vs Campaign Monitor: Complete Comparison
intro: ConvertKit and Campaign Monitor are both established email marketing platforms, but they serve different audiences. ConvertKit is purpose-built for creators and course sellers with a freemium model, while Campaign Monitor caters to agencies and teams needing multi-brand management with a paid-only structure. Understanding their strengths will help you choose the right tool for your business.

comparison_table.tool_a: Landing pages, commerce, visual automations, sequences
comparison_table.tool_b: Multi-brand management, journey designer, transactional email

choose_a_if: You're a creator, blogger, or online course seller looking for an affordable entry point
You need built-in landing pages, e-commerce features, and subscriber tagging
You want to start with a free plan and upgrade as you grow
choose_b_if: You manage multiple client brands or businesses from one platform
You need advanced journey design and transactional email capabilities
You prioritize white-label solutions and agency-focused features

faq.q: Is ConvertKit better than Campaign Monitor?
faq.a: It depends on your use case. ConvertKit excels for individual creators and offers better value with its freemium model and built-in commerce features. Campaign Monitor is superior for agencies managing multiple brands. ConvertKit has a higher G2 rating (4.4 vs 4.1), suggesting better overall satisfaction among its user base.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, ConvertKit or Campaign Monitor?
faq.a: ConvertKit is cheaper overall, starting free and beginning paid plans at $29/month, whereas Campaign Monitor requires payment from $11/month. However, Campaign Monitor may offer better value per feature for agencies managing multiple brands due to its multi-brand management capabilities.

faq.q: Can I switch from Campaign Monitor to ConvertKit?
faq.a: Yes, both platforms support email list imports, making migration straightforward. You can export your subscribers from Campaign Monitor and import them into ConvertKit. Automation workflows and templates will need to be recreated manually, so plan for some setup time depending on complexity.
